feed.title: EXYONE@BLOG:~$
feed.subtitle: Technical blog about .NET, front-end development, and web technologies. Sharing coding tutorials, software architecture insights, and development best practices.

entry.title: Hermes Agent: The Gold Standard for Code Quality and Token Efficiency in AI Agent Frameworks

link.title: Hermes Agent: The Gold Standard for Code Quality and Token Efficiency in AI Agent Frameworks

entry.summary: In the crowded landscape of AI agent frameworks, Hermes Agent stands out not for marketing hype, but for something far more fundamental: exceptional code quality and remarkable token efficiency. This expanded analysis draws from extensive research across GitHub repositories, community discussions, benchmarks, official documentation, and real-user comparisons as of mid-2026. We will delve deep...

entry.title: A View of Poland from a Chinese Perspective

link.title: A View of Poland from a Chinese Perspective

entry.summary: Polska część tego artykułu została wygenerowana przez tłumaczenie AI. Autor nie mówi po polsku. Polska część może zawierać wyrażenia, które nie są w pełni naturalne, i proszę polskich czytelników o wyrozumiałość. Początek: Polski przyjaciel na Bilibili Kilka tygodni temu trafiłem na Bilibili (druga co do wielkości platforma wideo w Chinach) na vlogera z Polski – Tatarskiego (jego strona). W ...

entry.title: AI Programming Advanced Guide for Non-Programmers: The Ultimate Tool Combo

link.title: AI Programming Advanced Guide for Non-Programmers: The Ultimate Tool Combo

entry.summary: In the previous article, we discussed how to open the command line, how to use configuration files to manage variable parameters, and how to use version control to protect your code. Those were foundational concepts aimed at getting you running and avoiding pitfalls. This article is an advanced “cookbook.” We won’t talk about broad choices—we’ll give you a hard-core combo that can take you thr...

entry.title: AI Programming for Non-Programmers: From Command Line to Natural Language Development

link.title: AI Programming for Non-Programmers: From Command Line to Natural Language Development

entry.summary: The spark for this article came from a conversation with a friend who has zero coding experience. They asked me: “AI is so advanced now, I want to use it to develop software.” I looked around at tutorials online and found they tend to fall into two extremes: some barely scratch the surface, just saying “just ask AI”; others immediately throw technical jargon that scares beginners away. In this...

entry.title: Adding Twikoo Comments to Chirpy: A Plugin Development Journey

link.title: Adding Twikoo Comments to Chirpy: A Plugin Development Journey

entry.summary: Chirpy is an excellent theme, but it doesn’t natively support Twikoo comments. I wanted to add Twikoo alongside the existing Giscus system without modifying the theme’s source code. The solution evolved through multiple iterations: I started with a Jekyll plugin, then tried footer.html template injection, and finally settled on a local-build-then-push workflow to ensure consistency across all d...
